vocab.meaning: người hành động độc lập, không tuân theo quy tắc chung
vocab.example: In primary school, I was a bit of a law unto myself, preferring to invent my own solo games rather than joining the typical playground sports.

vocab.meaning: rảnh rỗi, không có việc gì làm
vocab.example: Whenever I was at a loose end, I’d gather the neighborhood kids to organize an impromptu treasure hunt in the backyard.

vocab.meaning: rất thân thiết, luôn gắn bó với nhau
vocab.example: My cousins and I were thick as thieves, so I rarely did anything alone; we were always plotting our next adventure together.

vocab.meaning: bắt đầu học hỏi/thu được kinh nghiệm đầu đời từ việc gì
vocab.example: I cut my teeth on collaborative play by joining a local youth theater group, which taught me the value of teamwork at a very young age.

vocab.meaning: tự mình làm việc gì đó mà không cần sự giúp đỡ
vocab.example: I often chose to go it alone when it came to artistic hobbies like drawing, as I found other people's input rather distracting.
We used to have a dry run of our school plays in the garden, with all the children in the street taking on different roles.

vocab.word: be in a world of one's own
vocab.ipa: /bi ɪn ə wɜːrld əv wʌnz əʊn/
vocab.meaning: đắm chìm trong thế giới riêng của mình
vocab.example: I was often in a world of my own as a child, perfectly content to spend hours reading under a tree without any company.

vocab.word: know which side one's bread is buttered
vocab.ipa: /nəʊ wɪtʃ saɪd wʌnz bred ɪz ˈbʌtərd/
vocab.meaning: biết điều gì có lợi cho mình (để cư xử cho phù hợp trong nhóm)
vocab.example: I learned quickly how to stay on the good side of the older kids in our group; I knew which side my bread was buttered!

vocab.meaning: dẫn đầu, nắm quyền điều khiển
vocab.example: Even though I enjoyed group activities, I usually preferred to take the lead and decide which games we were going to play.

vocab.meaning: trở nên tự tin hoặc quen thuộc với môi trường mới
vocab.example: It took me a while to find my feet in large groups, but once I did, I became much more vocal and outgoing than when I was playing solo.
